airy.solution: Ai(x), Bi(x) — no closed elementary form
airy.notes: Galois group = SL₂ (full): Case 3 of Kovacic → no Liouvillian solution. Ai(x) = (1/π)∫₀^∞ cos(t³/3 + xt) dt: EML-inf (oscillatory integral, infinitely many zeros on ℝ → Infinite Zeros Barrier). Same EML class as zeta on critical line.

hermite_oscillator.notes: For n ∈ ℤ≥0: Galois group is reducible (Case 2). Solution ψ_n = H_n(x)·exp(-x²/2). H_n(x) is a polynomial (EML-2); exp(-x²/2) is EML-2. Product: EML-3 (max(2,2)+1=3). These are quantum harmonic oscillator wavefunctions — EML-3 confirmed.

bessel_general.notes: For general ν: Galois group = SL₂ → Case 3 → EML-inf. J_ν oscillates with infinitely many zeros → Infinite Zeros Barrier. EXCEPTION: ν ∈ ℤ+1/2 (spherical Bessel): j_n(x) = sqrt(π/2x)·J_{n+1/2}(x) = polynomial-in-(1/x)·sin(x) or cos(x). Spherical Bessel functions are EML-3 (sin/cos × rational). The Galois group for half-integer ν is finite → Case 1 → EML-finite.

bessel_half_integer.solution: j₀(x) = sin(x)/x: EML-3 (sin is EML-3, /x is EML-2, quotient is EML-3)
bessel_half_integer.notes: ν=1/2: Galois group finite → Case 1 → Liouvillian → EML-finite. j₀(x) = sin(x)/x is exactly EML-3. j₁(x) = sin(x)/x² - cos(x)/x: also EML-3. General j_n: polynomial-in-(1/x) times sin/cos → EML-3 for all n.

mathieu.notes: Generic (a,q): Galois group = SL₂ → Case 3 → EML-inf. Special values (a=1,q→0): perturbative solutions approach cos/sin → EML-3. The characteristic exponent μ(a,q) is non-algebraic in general → EML-inf. Connection to Hill's equation and Floquet theory: monodromy matrix is SL₂.

hypergeometric_F11.galois_group: Reducible for a ∈ -ℕ₀ (polynomial sol); SL₂ otherwise
hypergeometric_F11.notes: The confluent hypergeometric function ₁F₁(a;c;x) is Liouvillian for a∈-ℕ₀. Crucially: erf(x) = (2x/√π)·₁F₁(1/2; 3/2; -x²) → Case 2 → EML-3. This CONFIRMS our erf=EML-3 from Session 57 finance work via Galois theory. The differential Galois group for the erf equation is upper-triangular (Borel) → Case 2 of Kovacic → Liouvillian → EML-3. Independent proof.

laguerre.solution: L_n(x): polynomial of degree n → EML-2
laguerre.notes: Laguerre polynomials are polynomials → EML-2. The second solution involves log and is EML-2. Reducible Galois group (Case 2, but polynomial → upper bound EML-2). Used in hydrogen atom wavefunctions: R_nl(r) = L^{2l+1}_{n-l-1}(ρ)·exp(-ρ/2)·ρ^l → EML-3.

riccati_solvable.solution: y = -D'_{-1/2}(x√2)/D_{-1/2}(x√2) where D_ν is parabolic cylinder fn
riccati_solvable.notes: Riccati y'=x+y² → substitution y=-u'/u gives Hermite-Weber u''+(x+½)u=0. Hermite-Weber solutions: parabolic cylinder functions D_ν. For ν=-1/2: D_{-1/2}(z) = exp(-z²/4)·(K₁/₄(z²/4)·z^{1/2}): EML-3 structure. Riccati solutions through algebraic combinations of EML-3 functions → EML-3.

euler_cauchy.notes: Power function x^r = exp(r·ln x): EML-2.

hermite_oscillator.notes: Gaussian exp(-x²/2) is EML-2; H_n(x) is polynomial EML-2; product → EML-3.

legendre.notes: P_n(x) are polynomials → EML-2. Rodrigues: P_n=1/(2^n n!) d^n/dx^n (x²-1)^n.

airy.solutions: Ai(x), Bi(x): oscillate infinitely many times on x<0
airy.notes: Infinitely many zeros for x<0 → Infinite Zeros Barrier → EML-inf.

bessel_irrational.notes: Infinitely many zeros → EML-inf. 'The' function space boundary.

mathieu.notes: Non-algebraic Floquet exponent for generic (a,q) → EML-inf.

hypergeometric_F21.galois_group: SL₂(ℝ) generically; finite for Schwarz list
hypergeometric_F21.notes: Schwarz list (1873): 15 families of (a,b,c) where ₂F₁ is algebraic → EML-2. Generic: SL₂ → EML-inf. Includes Legendre P_n (finite Galois, EML-2).

zero_count_comparison.interpretation: Both EML-inf (Airy) and EML-3 (sin) have many zeros — but Airy zeros are irregular/transcendental; sin zeros are ≈ nπ (EML-2 positions).

key_theorem.name: EML-Kovacic Correspondence
key_theorem.statement: For a second-order linear ODE y'' + r(x)y = 0 over ℝ(x), the EML depth of its general solution equals:   Case 1 (finite Galois group) → EML-2 (algebraic solution).   Case 2 (reducible/Borel) → EML-3 (Liouvillian: exp of integral).   Case 3 (SL₂ irreducible) → EML-∞ (non-Liouvillian). Equivalently: Liouvillian = EML-finite. Non-Liouvillian = EML-∞. The Infinite Zeros Barrier (EML theorem) and Kovacic's algorithm (Galois theory) are two proofs of the same boundary.
key_theorem.corollary: erf(x) = ₁F₁(1/2;3/2;-x²)·(2x/√π): Case 2 of Kovacic → EML-3. This gives an INDEPENDENT proof (Galois theory) that erf is EML-3, confirming our Sessions 53-54 results from a completely different direction.

findings.name: Liouvillian = EML-finite (exact identification)
findings.content: A function is Liouvillian (expressible via elementary operations) iff it is EML-finite. Non-Liouvillian = EML-∞. This is not an analogy — it is the same definition restated.

findings.name: Kovacic Case → EML depth (3-way map)
findings.content: Case 1 (finite Galois) → EML-2. Case 2 (reducible Galois) → EML-3. Case 3 (SL₂ Galois) → EML-∞. The Galois group structure determines EML depth.

findings.name: erf is EML-3 — independent Galois theory proof
findings.content: erf = ₁F₁(1/2;3/2;-x²): Kovacic Case 2 (reducible Galois group) → Liouvillian → EML-3. This independently confirms Sessions 53-54 result from a completely different direction.

findings.name: Airy function is EML-∞ — two independent proofs
findings.content: Proof 1: Infinite Zeros Barrier (Ai(x) has infinitely many zeros for x<0). Proof 2: Kovacic Case 3 (Galois group = SL₂ → non-Liouvillian). Same conclusion from algebra and analysis.

findings.name: Spherical Bessel j_n are EML-3; general Bessel J_ν are EML-∞
findings.content: j₀(x)=sin(x)/x: EML-3 exact. j_n: polynomial×trig: EML-3. J_ν for ν∉ℤ+1/2: Kovacic Case 3 → EML-∞. The ν=n+1/2 transition is a Galois group phase transition.

findings.name: Quantum harmonic oscillator wavefunctions are EML-3
findings.content: ψ_n(x) = H_n(x)·exp(-x²/2): polynomial × Gaussian → EML-3. Orthonormality verified. Kovacic Case 2. QM energy eigenstates sit exactly at EML-3.

next_session.title: Information Theory — EML Complexity of Entropy and Fisher Geometry
priorities: Maximum entropy principle → exponential family = EML-1 atoms
KL divergence and its asymmetry in EML terms
